summary: Analyze is a one-shot overview (à la Wolfram Alpha): it shows several views of an expression at once — simplified, expanded, factored, its derivative, integral, and real roots.
method: Type any expression (a single unknown is detected automatically).
The tool computes a panel of standard views rather than one transformation.
Read each card: simplified and expanded forms, the factored form, the derivative and integral, and the real roots.
Use it to get oriented, then switch to a specific operation for the full step-by-step.

example.title: Analyze a quadratic
example.find: an overview of its key forms
steps: Real roots of x² − 4 = 0: x = −2 and x = 2.
